research_findings: The committee design already names delivery and audit secretary roles, but their output is still implicit in scope_decision and flat summaries.
The smallest durable improvement is to add explicit secretariat slots to review_state and teach capture-review.py to write them.
This version should stop at schema plus capture path; reports and snapshots can consume the new fields in a later iteration.
committee_feedback: Product Council: explicit secretary output will make the convergence step auditable instead of inferred.
Architecture Council: the new fields should stay narrow and fit the existing review_state shape.
Operator Council: keep secretary capture structured and low-ceremony so it does not become transcript storage.

research_gate.summary: Persist explicit delivery-secretary and audit-secretary outputs in review_state and capture-review.py.

product_council.summary: Make the convergence layer explicit in durable state so later reports and tooling can reference it cleanly.
product_council.decision: Approve schema and capture-path work only; defer visibility changes to the next version.

architecture_council.summary: Add normalized secretariat slots to review_state and extend capture-review.py with explicit flags for each secretary role.
architecture_council.decision: Keep delivery and audit artifacts compact and compatible with existing scope_decision fields.

operator_council.summary: The new fields should improve traceability without adding mandatory ceremony to every operator action.
operator_council.decision: Approve if capture-review.py can write secretary output in the same lightweight style as current council summaries.

delivery_secretary.summary: Converged the councils on one narrow step: make secretary output durable before expanding where it is rendered.
delivery_secretary.next_action: Validate the new review_state.secretariat schema and publish it as a compatible extension.

audit_secretary.summary: Record explicit secretary summaries, evidence refs, open gaps, and dissent in review_state without replacing scope_decision.
audit_secretary.decision_record: The convergence and audit outputs now need dedicated durable fields so later tools can read them directly.
open_gaps: Reports and project-data snapshots do not render secretariat output yet.
dissent_record: Do not let the new secretariat fields drift into verbose meeting notes.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Persist explicit delivery and audit secretary outputs in review state
scope_decision.why_selected: The secretary roles are already part of the architecture, but their output is still implicit and therefore harder to inspect or reuse.
scope_in: Add secretariat slots to normalized review_state.
Teach capture-review.py to write delivery-secretary and audit-secretary artifacts.
Cover the new fields in validation and templates.
scope_out: Do not render secretariat output in reports or project-data snapshots yet.
Do not change readiness or publication gates.
stop_conditions: Stop if secretariat capture starts duplicating entire scope decisions or full narrative transcripts.
dissent: Keep the secretariat schema compact; it should store conclusions, not meeting minutes.
scope_decision.next_action: Use the new durable secretariat fields in reports and project snapshots in the next version.

critique: This closes an audit gap in the committee model without broadening scope into new rendering or gate behavior.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep the secretariat fields compact and compatible with the existing scope-decision contract.

review_state.goal_title: Persist explicit delivery and audit secretary outputs in review state
